title: Tongo
description: A confidential payment protocol on Starknet that uses ElGamal homomorphic encryption and zero-knowledge proofs to enable private ERC20 token transfers. Account balances are stored as encrypted ciphertexts on-chain, hiding amounts while keeping the protocol verifiable and supporting an optional auditor for regulatory compliance.

properties.name: Confidentiality
properties.notes: Tongo uses ElGamal homomorphic encryption to store account balances as encrypted ciphertexts. Each account maintains two balance types: current (balance that the user can use to send transactions) and pending (balance that the user has received but cannot use to send transactions yet. The user has to explicitly move funds from pending to current. This mechanism prevents balance attacks). Both stored as ciphertexts, making it impossible for external observers to see any amount. Mathematical operations on balances are performed without ever decrypting them.

properties.name: Anonymity
properties.notes: Tongo uses an account-based model where each account is identified by a public key. Transfers are sent from one account's public key to another, meaning both the sender and receiver addresses are visible on-chain when a Tongo transaction is submitted. The documentation does not mention any anonymity method that could be used with the protocol

properties.name: Asset privacy
properties.notes: When funding a Tongo account, the user deposits a specific ERC20 token into the contract. When withdrawing from a Tongo account, the smart contract sends the specific ERC20 token to the user. When transacting between Tongo accounts, the transaction shows the ERC20 token being send but the amount and balances are encrypted. Therefore there is no asset privacy in any of the 3 interactions: funding, withdrawing or transacting.

properties.name: Plausible deniability
properties.notes: Tongo interactions (funding, withdrawing and transacting) are direct calls to the Tongo smart contract, making it publicly visible that users are interacting with the privacy protocol. There is no way a user can plausible deny using the protocol or sending tokens to a Tongo account. The amount being transferred is the only value that a user could plausible deny.

properties.name: Number of secrets
properties.notes: Users require a Starknet wallet (secret #1) to submit on-chain transactions and a separate Tongo account key pair used for ElGamal encryption and decryption of their private balances (secret #2). The Tongo account can be created independently from the Starknet wallet depending on the UI implementation.

properties.name: Time-to-finality
properties.notes: Tongo is deployed on Starknet, a ZK-rollup L2 on Ethereum. Starknet's time to L1 finality is approximately 2 hours (120 minutes), as STARK proofs must be aggregated and verified on Ethereum L1. Adding the Ethereum blocktime (0.2 minutes) gives approximately 120 minutes total.

properties.name: Deposit time
properties.notes: No mandatory deposit waiting period. Funding a Tongo account is an immediate Starknet transaction.

properties.name: Withdraw time
properties.notes: No mandatory withdrawal waiting period. Users can withdraw their full balance at any time, including via the instant ragequit mechanism.

properties.name: Censorship resistance
properties.notes: The docs talk about different compliance mechanisms that can be configured at deployment. It looks like Tongo takes the auditor path where a third party can examine transactions (real time monitoring, selective disclosure, retroactive investigations) but not censor a transaction or account. The advanced features talk about zero knowledge compliance features that include whitelist compliance which could be used to censor addresses on that specific Tongo instance.

properties.name: External network dependence
properties.notes: Tongo requires no trusted setup ceremony. The protocol's cryptography relies solely on the discrete logarithm assumption over the Stark curve, eliminating the need for any external trusted parties or multi-party computation ceremonies. All operations are self-contained within the Starknet smart contracts.

properties.name: Escape hatch
properties.notes: Tongo provides a ragequit mechanism that allows users to instantly withdraw their full encrypted balance back to the underlying ERC20 tokens at any time. All funds in the current balance are send to the user public address, the pending balance is not manipulated in this operation.

properties.name: Upgradeability
properties.notes: The Tongo smart contracts do not implement any proxy pattern or upgrade mechanism. Core parameters such as the conversion rate, bit size, and wrapped ERC20 address are immutable once deployed. The only administrative function available is rotating the auditor public key, which is restricted to the contract owner.

properties.name: Client-side proving
properties.notes: Tongo uses homomorphic encryption (ElGamal) and zero knowledge proofs to update the on-chain encrypted balances. The SDK contains prover functions that can be used on the user end devices. There is no mention of external proving services being use for speed or scalability optimization purposes.

properties.name: Third-party inspectability
properties.notes: A global auditor (set by the contract owner at deployment time) could decrypt balances for regulation and compliance purposes. The auditor feature is activated at deployment and cannot be added after it. The global auditor keys can be distributed across multiple parties to improve decentralization.

properties.name: Implementation maturity
properties.notes: Tongo has been deployed on Starknet mainnet for less than one year. The implementation passed a security audit by ZKSECURITY.

properties.name: Post-quantum secure
properties.notes: Tongo's security relies on ElGamal encryption over elliptic curves. This scheme is vulnerable to quantum computing attacks via Shor's algorithm, making the protocol not post-quantum secure. The harvest-now-decrypt-later problem is present in this privacy protocol.

properties.name: Layer of enforcement
properties.notes: Compliance is enforced at the application layer through different optional methods that can be activated at deployment time: the global auditor mechanism that allow viewing access to authorities, the range compliance method that prove transfer amounts are below threshold, the velocity limits mechanism that proves cumulative amounts are within bounds and the whitelist compliance method that proves a recipient is in a authorized addresses list.

properties.name: Enforcement entities
properties.notes: The Tongo contract owner is the enforcement entity, responsible for configuring the auditor public key. The auditor role is a designated party (single key or threshold key encryption) that can examine transactions and potentially block addresses from using the protocol.

properties.name: Type of compliance
properties.notes: Tongo main compliance feature is the global auditor that can examine encrypted balances and transfer amounts. The advanced compliance features also allow for range compliance (transfer amounts less than N), velocity limits (maximum number of transfers per time period) and whitelist compliance (authorized addresses to use the protocol)

properties.name: Point of enforcement
properties.notes: The auditor can examine balances or amounts at all stages of the transaction lifecycle: deposits (funding), internal transfers, and withdrawals. The other advance compliance features (range compliance, velocity limits and whitelist compliance) can be implemented at any step of the lifecycle.

properties.name: Selective disclosure: viewing entity
properties.notes: Tongo supports two viewing entity types: the auditor can view transaction details without explicit user consent (involuntary third-party disclosure), and users can also voluntarily disclose their transaction history to any third party.

properties.name: Selective disclosure: viewing control
properties.notes: Selective disclosure control (global auditor) is set at deployment. If the user wants he can proof a specific transaction detail to a third party without revealing their private keys. This mechanism is called Ex-Post proving and described in the compliance section of the documentation.

properties.name: Cryptographic verifiability
properties.notes: Tongo uses ElGamal homomorphic encryption combined with zero-knowledge proofs to ensure that all operations (fund, transfer, withdrawal) are cryptographically verifiable. The protocol leverages Starknet's native elliptic curve operations for efficient on-chain verification.

properties.name: Open source
properties.notes: The Tongo protocol smart contracts (Cairo) and TypeScript SDK are publicly available on GitHub under the fatlabsxyz organization.

properties.name: Scalable state
properties.notes: Tongo uses an account-based model where each user's encrypted balance is stored as a mapping within the smart contract storage. New users add entries to the contract's account mapping, and the state grows proportionally to the number of accounts rather than to the number of transactions.

properties.name: Client-side indexing
properties.notes: Because Tongo uses an account-based model with balances stored directly as contract state, users can retrieve their encrypted balance directly from the contract without scanning past blockchain events or transactions.

properties.name: Private state model
properties.notes: Tongo maintains an account-based private state where each account holds an encrypted current balance (immediately spendable) and an encrypted pending balance (received but not yet rolled over). This mechanism prevents spam attacks to balances that could block senders from executing transfer transactions

properties.name: On-chain storage
properties.notes: All private state—including encrypted account balances (current and pending) and auditor configuration—is stored directly in the Tongo smart contract on Starknet. The state grows for every new Tongo account generated.

properties.name: Access to DeFi
properties.notes: Tongo is a payment-focused protocol with no native DeFi integrations. The four core operations (fund, transfer, rollover, withdrawal) are all payment primitives. Users must withdraw from Tongo to interact with any external DeFi protocol.

properties.name: Programmability / Generality
properties.notes: Tongo is designed exclusively as a confidential payment system.
